title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest on March 28 at Boston Common, part of over 160 events in Massachusetts.
Emily Rubenstein watched and cheered at the rally's screen, while Marcelo Gomes Da Silva spoke to the crowd.
Protesters displayed signs with messages like 'No Kings Then 1776; No Kings Now 2026' at the Boston Common rally.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: 'No Kings' movement sparks protests across Europe, with rallies in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam on March 28, 2026.
The 'No Kings Day' protests criticize Donald Trump, coinciding with over 3,000 planned protests across the U.S., including Massachusetts.
Thousands join the 'No Kings' protests in solidarity with U.S. movements, marking a significant mobilization against Trump's presidency.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
Cast members include Alicia Carmody, a Providence restaurateur, and Liz McGraw, owner of a cannabis dispensary.
The series explores Rhode Island's social scene, focusing on friendships, feuds, and family ties.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department to cut about 50 jobs to save up to $4 million.
Budget cuts removed $26.5 million from the department's fiscal year 2026 budget.
Reduced patrols in Springfield, Holyoke, Palmer; cut three analyst roles in Chicopee.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council approved $2.65 million to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals linked to health risks.
The new funding will provide over 270 firefighters with two sets of PFAS-free protective gear each.
Massachusetts law will ban the sale of firefighting gear with PFAS starting January following recent health concerns.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley discussed his documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' at Concord Carlisle Regional High School on March 23.
The Walden Woods Project, founded by Henley in 1990, aims to preserve the legacy of Henry David Thoreau.
The documentary airs March 30-31 on PBS, featuring narrations by George Clooney and Jeff Goldblum.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics attended a friendly soccer match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium for a team outing.
France won 2-1, benefiting from a goal by Kylian Mbappe, with Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez swapping jerseys with him.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla drew parallels between basketball and soccer, emphasizing possession and counterattacks.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored a game-high 36 points, helping the Celtics defeat the Atlanta Hawks 109-102.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points and 12 rebounds despite struggling with his shooting accuracy in the game.
Jordan Walsh returned to action, providing defensive support with three blocks and scoring five points.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles' tendon, driven by the team's championship contention.
The Celtics hold second place in the standings, with playoffs on the horizon, influencing Tatum's decision to rejoin the team.
Tatum's recovery is ongoing; he's shooting below career averages but excels in rebounding, averaging 9.2 per game.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard, officials confirmed.
The incident involved an altercation between two armed men known to each other in the building, authorities reported.
The second man involved remained at the scene and the investigation is ongoing by multiple law enforcement agencies.
